member.name: Dr Allison Gardner
member.synopsis: Dr Allison Gardner is the Labour MP for Stoke-on-Trent South , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Epilepsy: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of the level of funding for epilepsy research.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department delivers research via the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). Between 2020/21 and 2024/25, the NIHR invested £12.8 million in direct research funding on epilepsy. This investment in epilepsy research allows us to develop...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Epilepsy: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to increase the number of GPs and nurses with specialist training in epilepsy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of ensuring that healthcare professionals are appropriately trained to identify and manage epilepsy.General practitioners and nurses receive training in neurological conditions, including epilepsy, as part of their...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Freehold
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to his Department's consultation entitled Enhanced protections for homeowners on freehold estates, published on 18 December 2025, when he expects to publish legisl
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 128335 on 27 April 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Surgical Mesh Implants: Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what progress his Department has made on compensating people with pelvic mesh injury sufferers; and what his planned timeline is for delivering that compensation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is carefully considering the work done by the Patient Safety Commissioner and her report, which set out recommendations for redress for those harmed by valproate and pelvic mesh.The Government has deep sympathy for all those affected and re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what progress her Department has made on implementing the findings of the Farming Profitability Review 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Baroness Batters’ Review offers a clear assessment of the challenges alongside 57 recommendations for strengthening farm businesses. We are carefully considering the findings and recommendations with the sector to support farmers to access the tools and o...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her department is taking to support British farming profitability.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our Environmental Land Management schemes are strengthening the environmental foundations of farm profitability, and the Farming and Food Partnership Board will drive long-term profitability across the farming sector. Furthermore, our response to Barones...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Diabetes: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what plans his department has to increase the uptake of annual diabetes health checks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting people with diabetes. Improving the uptake of annual diabetes health checks recommended by National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) is a key primary care metric in the NHS Oversight Framework, whic...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Diabetes: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his department is taking to improve access to finger-prick blood glucose testing for Type 1 Diabetes when symptoms first appear.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As I set out in the debate on Type 1 Diabetes: Infant Testing in response to petition 728677 on Monday 9 March 2026, the Government is committed to supporting people with type 1 diabetes. The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence has recently ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Diabetes: Health Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his department is taking to improve awareness and understanding of Type 1 Diabetes across the Health and Social Care system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As I reaffirmed in the Westminster Hall debate on type 1 diabetes on 9 March 2026, the Government is committed to improving awareness of type 1 diabetes. NHS England carried out communications activity to coincide with World Diabetes Day in November 2025,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Diabetes: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his department is taking to help ensure the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) recommended diabetes care guidelines are adhered to.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) guidelines provide recommendations on best practice in terms of both the effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of interventions and services. Guidelines describe best practice and National Health...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Urinary Tract Infections
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure the psychological impacts of chronic urinary tract infections are recognised.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department and NHS England recognise the psychological impact of chronic urinary tract infections (UTIs). NHS England published the Excellence in Continence Care framework on 23 July 2018, which is available at the following link:https://www.england.n...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Urinary Tract Infections: Bacteriophages
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of phage medicines on tackling (a) recurrent and chronic urinary tract infections and (b) associated antimicrobial resistance.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department recognises bacteriophage, or phage, medicines as a promising complementary option for difficult bacterial infections, including recurrent and chronic urinary tract infections (UTIs), and as a potential tool to address antimicrobial resistan...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Urinary Tract Infections
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department is taking steps with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence to introduce a definition for chronic urinary tract infections.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) is the independent body responsible for developing evidence‑based guidance for the National Health Service on best practice in the care and treatment of patients with specific conditions.NICE do...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Urinary Tract Infections: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of medical (a) gender inequality and (b) misogyny on the (i) diagnostics and (ii) treatment of urinary tract infections.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a specific assessment. The National Institute for Clinical Excellence’s (NICE) guidelines, such as those for the diagnosis and management of urinary tract infections, are subject to equality impact assessments as part of NICE’s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Urinary Tract Infections: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to support research on vaccinations against recurrent and chronic urinary tract infections.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds research through the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). The NIHR funds, supports, and carries out high-quality research to improve health outcomes and care services. Over the last five financial years, the Departm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Urinary Tract Infections: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential merits of using uropathogen infection testing for diagnosing urinary tract infections.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a specific assessment. There are several sources of guidance regarding the diagnosis of urinary tract infections (UTIs), including from the National Institute for Clinical Excellence and the UK Health Security Agency.The TOUCAN...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Menopause: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent progress has been made on incorporating menopause screening into NHS Health Checks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are currently working with experts, including general practitioners, to co-design the menopause content for the NHS Health Check.The inclusion of menopause in the NHS Health Check will support eligible women across England to access high quality inform...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Forensic Science Regulator has established a working group to develop regulatory requirements around the use of drug testing kits and devices. The working group involves police, forensic providers, academia and government organisations, including the ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Forensic Science Regulator has established a working group to develop regulatory requirements around the use of drug testing kits and devices. The working group involves police, forensic providers, academia and government organisations, including the ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Synthetic Cathinones: Misuse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the potential merits of updating the Home Office Approved Field Testing guidance in relation to synthetic cathinones.
